neighborhoods.name: Wexford Town Centre
neighborhoods.description: Medieval port town with narrow Viking-founded streets, the National Opera House, and bustling quayside
neighborhoods.character: Medieval port, opera culture, compact walkable

housing.predominantType: Townhouse apartments and renovated historic buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Wexford. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Rosslare / Rosslare Harbour
neighborhoods.description: Coastal area with blue flag beaches, ferry port to Wales and France, and holiday homes

housing.predominantType: Holiday homes, bungalows, and coastal apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Wexford's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Enniscorthy
neighborhoods.description: Inland market town on the River Slaney with Norman castle, affordable housing, and 1798 heritage
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Gorey
neighborhoods.description: Thriving north Wexford town with excellent amenities, new housing estates, and Courtown beach nearby
neighborhoods.character: Growing town, excellent amenities, beach access

housing.predominantType: Modern estate houses and newer apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
